feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Is it possible to see a report output of login history to IAS/IPS?
entry.content:
Hi,

We are migrating from SuccessFactors to IAS.
Currently, SuccessFactors uses a table report (Domain: Login Data) to view login history to SuccessFactors for auditing purposes.

After applying IAS/IPS, we need to check login history to IAS/IPS in the same way.
Is there any way to check who accessed the system and when?
If so, I would like to know how to do so.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Email address conversion process when syncing from SuccessFactors to IPS
entry.content:
Hi,
We are in the process of transitioning from SuccessFactors to IAS.
We are aware that when synchronizing from SuccessFactors to IPS, if the same dummy address is set, we can set a unique address on IPS/IAS by specifying that dummy address.
(https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/568fdf1f14f14fd089a3cd15194d19cc/f596827269d242e1b5d32ecb1d826cba.html? locale=en-US)

However, is there any way to prepare for the case where multiple users have duplicate real email addresses instead of the same dummy address?
In operation, there is a possibility that the same real e-mail address may be set for multiple users.

Best Regards.

SAP SuccessFactors HXM Core 
SAP Cloud Identity Services 
Identity Provisioning

entry.title: Using BTP IPS, is it possible to read data from tables or else other function modules for ABAP ?
entry.content:
Hello All,

I would like to understand Using IPS, is it possible to read data from standard tables or else other function modules? I remember using SAP IDM ABAP connector it is possible to read data from tables as well as execute function modules apart from the standard BAPI_USER_CHANGE or RFC_READ_TABLE etc.,

My requirement is to execute RFC enabled BAPI calls like RFC_READ_TABLE/RSAU_READ_LOG and expose the data as the Proxy api. Currently i am able to connect SAP ABAP NW 7.5 system to IPS as a proxy and able to read the roles, users and perform provisioning activities related to user using the Proxy api's .

entry.title: SAML 2.0 Signing certificate renewal for Ariba Sourcing- service provider certificate
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
we received a notification for SP provider certificate renewal for Ariba sourcing. We are using SAP IAS as IdP, and I tried to renew the certificate on Ariba side, but there is no way to do so , same for creating a new outbound connection, but certificate validity is same as the old one. How do I renew the signing certificate? as it then done by Ariba team? because I am not able to find a way to do so. 
also is there a way to remove / delete the outbound connection created in Ariba? 

 

 



Thanks and best regards,
Imtiaz

entry.title: How to set default user parameter for sap s/4hana private edit via SAP IPS
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are using SAP ips to provision user from azure AD to our SAP s/4hana private edition.

One of our business processes required default user parameters (as maintained on su3/su01).

We tried to find any documentation how to set it on the transformation but not find anything.

Does anyone maybe know how to do it?

Thank in advance.

SAP Cloud Identity Services 

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

entry.title: Source Transformation for IAS
entry.content:
Hello,

we are trying to connect IAS to FSM via IPS, IAS source transformation pulls complete user and groups from IAS, so we added attribute ias.group.filter = displayName eq 'FSM' to restrict group and in source transformation below condition :

{
"user": {
"condition": "$.groups[?(@.value == 'FSM')] EMPTY false",
"mappings": [

Groups are correctly pulled but all users within that group are skipped.

entry.title: No entity found in FSM - Error log - SAP FSM
entry.content:
Hello,

we are trying to connect IAS to FSM via IPS for user provisioning and are receiving below message in IPS log :

error=No entity found by https://us.coresystems.net/api/scim/v2/Groups?filter=displayName eq "YTEST"

YTEST is the group and its already created in FSM admin policy and IAS.

User are getting created in FSM but groups are not getting mapped to the users.

entry.title: Corp IDP with Open ID Connect
entry.content:
Hi All,

in IAS, Can you please suggest if OpenID Connect option set for CorporateIDP exempts the SAML Configuration for the same.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: SSO RISE SAP
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

my client is looking for implementing SSO in SAPGUI in a RISE Cloud Solution by using Secure Login Service through SAP IAS.

After taking a look through several SAP articles and guides on internet, I have nor been able to locate a clear guide to implement such a solution.

May any of you have access to a document or something similar to guide me through this process?

Thanks in advance.

Best regards.

entry.title: Some employees are not being synced to IAS/IPS from SuccessFactors, not included on skip/error log
entry.content:
Hello,

Some employees are not being synced to IAS/IPS and do not appear on the error or skip logs. There are no filters set to exclude these users, their data in SuccessFactors is unique, and we do not have IAS for ONB enabled. Has anyone encountered this issue? If so, what was the fix? Any insight on what may be the cause is greatly appreciated!

Thank you,

Sydnie

entry.title: Is it possible to create a new user (not admin) from the CDC (Customer Data Cloud) portal manually?
entry.content:
Hi there,
I am trying to create a user manually from the CDC (Customer Data Cloud) portal. I can easily create admin users and add privilege to the admin user. But can't seem to find an option to add a new normal user and put that user in a group that I have created.

In CIS (Cloud Identity Service) it is straight forward and easy to create a new user and assign the user in a group. But can't seem to find similar thing in CDC.
It would be much appreciated if anyone points me to the right direction.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Datasphere IDP, IAS protocol support?
entry.content:
We can switch some BTP Services to use OIDC with IAS, a simpler method than Saml. However, SAP Datasphere seems limited to Saml. Is this because it's built on older tech not supporting OIDC?
I reviewed this link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/9b26536159354IAS, OIDCaea9024a99cbbe60b4e.html?q=sap%20datasphere%20SAC

Action & Inquiry

  • Seeking to confirm if anyone has successfully integrated IAS OIDC with SAP Datasphere.
  • Advise on next steps for using IAS OIDC for SAP Datasphere.

Appreciate any guidance,

Wallace

entry.title: How to get an audit log of more than 1000 accesses using Postman
entry.content:
I would like to know how to get an audit log of more than 1000 accesses using Postman.
I used the KBA instructions below to get the audit log data.
https://me.sap.com/notes/2913940

However, they were only able to obtain 1000 accesses of data in a single run.

I want to get more than 1000 data. Can you tell me how to remove the upper limit?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Protect service in cloud foundry using cloud identity service
entry.content: Having in sap cloud foundry a java application with the address X without any authentication, and having a sap cloud identity services. How can make X visible only with an authentication system made of sap identity cloud? How can I configure it step by step? There is a guide and a best approach? thanks

entry.title: SAML SSO and those lovely exceptions!
entry.content:
Hey there, fiends of the well-groomed SSO world!

So, here I am, fancying myself quite the expert on the subject, but hey, who says there's no room for a little community chitchat, right? This time it's about SAML and its implications on an SAP system. All of this in connection with those scenarios that are not in the sense of "user opens the launchpad".

Had a classic case at a client's place today. Just after introducing SAML, bam! Developers screaming bloody murder, their test scripts getting served up a delicious 401 error from the development environment.

We're talking about accesses from specific user groups on the system who:

  • Need to do a bit of coding (cue the /sap/opu),
  • For whatever reason, must, want, or should work with passwords (who knew we'd still need those, right?),
  • Require login with test user credentials, like your friendly neighborhood power users and app testers,

...and whatever else you can think of!

Question: So, how do you folks tackle configuring your ICF services or defining exceptions for these situations?

Give the info below a quick read, and let's dish out some helpful, tried-and-true advice. Oh, and a lively exchange of ideas wouldn't hurt either, if that's still a thing around here!

Here's the backstory

All SAP systems (ABAP, essentially meaning S/4HANA, possibly residing on ECS) at CUSTOMER have been configured to utilize SAML. This configuration is applied system-wide (and per client) and impacts every ICF service. This setup achieves Single Sign-On for HTTP access. Authentication is no longer handled by the S/4HANA system itself (Basic Password Authentication), but instead, it is forwarded via SAML to SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS), which delegates authentication to the Entra ID at CUSTOMER.

This facilitates seamless and secure access to web-based applications such as SAP Fiori Launchpad, WEBGUI, SOAMANAGER, NWBC, and others, without users needing their SAP password anymore. Instead, employees simply authenticate with their CUSTOMER account, and their identity is federated to the user in the SAP system (SU01 email address) via their email address.

There are also other scenarios, such as developers accessing specific services with hardcoded user credentials, using test scripts, or other cases where an exception is needed. In such instances, SAML can be selectively disabled to no longer delegate authentication to the Identity Provider via HTTP redirect/post for that specific service.

To address these requirements, the following measures can be implemented:

  • If individual ICF services need to be accessed without SAML authentication, for instance, in the context of test scripts during development, developers can append the URL-parameter ?|&saml2=disabled when making the call. This bypasses the SAML authentication process for that ICF node, switching to Basic Password Authentication, enabling normal login with user credentials in the SAP backend. Note: This approach might not work as expected in a BTP/Cloud Connector scenario.
  • If certain services don't require SAML authentication at all, this can be achieved through SICF configuration. SAML authentication can be disabled in the login procedures to ensure these services can be used without the SAML protocol.

Oh, and if you're eager for more bedtime stories, check out these links:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/ui5-app-in-scp-how-to-disable-saml/qaq-p/12301269

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002577263

I stumbled upon this one yesterday: The requirement is that all web-based logins should be compelled to use SAML, and anyone attempting to use 'saml2=disabled' should be denied or How to enforce all web-based logins to use SAML2 in an ABAP system: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003280746

Thx & Cheers
Carsten

entry.title: Cloud Identity Services - Connectivity plan for 3 tier system landscape
entry.content:
In order to connect IPS to an on-premise ABAP system we need to setup a dedicated subaccount of the IAS connectivity plan service. As outlined in the following document: 

"You can have two separate Cloud Foundry subaccounts per region: one of them configured for Productive connections and the other for Test connections. While it is allowed to create multiple Cloud Foundry subaccounts per one region and per one type (test or productive), be aware that in this case only the first one where the connectivity plan was enabled will be used by Identity Provisioning."

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/connecting-to-on-premise-systems-in-sap-cloud-identity-infrastructure

My question: How can I make this work in a 3-tier system landscape consisting of 1 prod and 2 non-prod environments? We recently purchased an additional SuccessFactors system that will act as a sandbox in addition to the already existing QA/Dev system. The new SuccessFactors system comes with its own IAS/IPS system. It does not appear like the 2nd IPS system is able to connect to the existing connectivity plan subaccount even though it is currently used by the other non-prod system with the same Region, customer ID, and Type (Test))

Is the only way to move the new "sandbox SF" system to a different region?

entry.title: IPS Transformation Azure AD (MemberOf) to ABAP
entry.content:
HI , 

I'm trying to transform memberof value from Azure AD .

i have set the aad.user.attributes.membership.active to true

I'm also configure the aad.group.attributes to id,displayName,mailNickname,members,description

in the source system i configured the groups at the user section

{
"sourcePath": "$.groups",
"targetPath": "$.groups",
"optional": false,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
},

in the target system I'm trying to set a user condition based on group membership 

"user": {

"condition": "$.groups[*].displayName =~ /.*S42_205.*/",

but the condition is not working , I'm not getting an error message but users are not created .

im already using groups to assign a role in the target system . its works well .

only when I'm trying to set a condition in the user section its not worked .

i saw in the documentation the json output 

"groups": [
{
"displayName": "Azure AD Group 1",
"id": "aaa111999-0000-444-123-777fff000",
"type": "group"
}
]

what I'm missing ???

entry.title: How to do a initial load of roles from source system with SAP IdDS as target?
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP IBP or any other SAP Cloud application should be provisioned with users and their group assignments from an external IAM tool.

The external IAM solution should push users and role assignments (for IBP) via SCIM 2.0 to SAP IdDS.

SAP IdDS pushs the users and role/group assignments via IPS to IBP.
But I don't know who the 3rd party IAM tool gets all the existing groups/roles from IBP.

How do I get the IBP groups into IdDS. The jobs in IPS source system only reads users and existing group assignments.

Where is the initial load job for all existing groups? Do I need a separate initial load job from 3rd party IAM tool via SCIM directly to IBP?

 

Best regards

Klaus

entry.title: Is there any impact from the addition of additional NAT and LB IP addresses?
entry.content:
We have been notified that additional NAT and LB IP addresses will be added.
However, I am not confident in how to verify the region.

With the addition of this IP address, will it be necessary to add a whitelist with IPSADMIN basic authentication?

I would like to know which applications this change will affect.
Please let me know.

Kind regards,
Koko

entry.title: How to recover password for users who don't have email address
entry.content:
现状：密码过期时只能通过邮箱去改密码，在网页上没有设置新密码的地方

要求：可以在登录的网页上去修改过期密码，因为公司有些人没有邮箱

Current situation: When the password expires, you can only change the password through the mailbox, and there is no place to set a new password on the page

Requirements: You can change the expired password on the login page, because some people in the company do not have email


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
